original text:
Soon after started internship Caster, realized that all the and public relations classes I’d taken didn’t teach everything that would come across real-world environment. Here was introduced new types projects and industry resources, and one thing I’ve done lot create media lists. didn’t know anything about media lists before being intern Caster, and had never learned how put one together. first instinct about media lists was that they were Word documents you created with media sources that pertained the topic hand. had idea how detailed they needed and how much useful information you could actually find. Luckily for have great team that eased into this process and taught how correctly put one together.
Media lists are compilation names, information and sources that are applicable campaigns and outreach for media pitching. These names include specific journalists and outlets who have written currently write about the topic you want pitch. For example, you’re looking for coverage about new tech gadget your client has released, you may want pitch those who write for TechCrunch Engadget, who covered similar product the past. The media list will usually consist the name journalist, outlet they write for, email contact information, Twitter handle and link something relevant they’ve written. has taken little while figure out which targets are good for certain pitch and should added list. I’ve learned that you want your target post regularly about the topic you are pitching. they have only posted about certain topic once, you’re better off finding something else. your target has large following and respected outlet the industry you are working in, that also good sign.
important key compiling good media list making sure that all the information you use up-to-date. sure that the journalist you include still working for the outlet and writing about topics that are your area interest. Another important detail watch out for the journalists’ email address, since addresses that are news@site.com and are just for general use and are ineffective for pitching. resource that use find this information Meltwater, which has search function where you can look for journalists. they are the system, Meltwater provides you with updated information for that journalist. someone you are looking for not Meltwater you must search through the web. suggest Googling their name and checking Twitter, LinkedIn and their personal sites for contact information.
Media lists are crucial part pitching help your clients get coverage. keep working media lists, become better and better. has become lot easier for spot good targets and have even begun recognize big names the tech-writing business. It’s great feeling knowing that your research being used effective way help get client coverage. Keep mind the tips and tricks shared above from personal experience and let know what you think connecting with Twitter @AliCerasuolo!

original text:
The Centre for the Development Christian Ministry Trinity Theological College (TTC) which organises courses equip Christians for lifelong learning, personal growth and ministry will offering two evening courses from September November 2016.
This historical survey the Reformation. The course traces the circumstances that gave rise the Reformation and the different religious, political and cultural forces that coalesced birthing the Protestant church. will examine the lives and legacies some the prominent reformers and the implications Karl Barth's comment that "the church always reformed" (Ecclesia semper reformanda est) for our present times.
Instructor: Rev. Dr. Andrew Peh faculty Trinity Theological College and lectures the area mission and mission history. alumni the college (TTC) and Asbury Theological Seminary and his interests are the history Christianity Asia and mission trends the 21st century. also serves the Chinese Annual Conference the Methodist Church Singapore diaconal minister appointed Charis Methodist Church.
Various ethical issues challenge Christians today: some result the increased and pervasiveness technology, others are dilemmas which arise because Christian values are variance with the world’s. Since these issues are often contentious and divisive, Christians need develop theological framework able salt and light society, with posture humility and grace articulating the Christian view. This course will give the opportunity and grow this knowledge and develop these skills.
Instructor: Dr. Kwa Kiem Kiok teaches East Asia School Theology the areas Intercultural Studies well ethics. She has worked both para-church organisations well her home church, Trinity Methodist Church. When she's not working, she enjoys listening classical music and reading detective novels.
For more information, please visit contact Esther Lee telephone +65 67613624 Email cdcm@ttc.sg. register, click here.

original text:
Today would like talk about yet another unique embroidery tradition the Dnister corridor, This river separates the historic districts Pokuttia and Podillia. This region incredibly rich embroidery techniques. This technique mostly known for being used the ends the ancient woman's headpiece, the peremitka, which long narrow piece linen which wound around the head and neck married women. The ends this garment were always decorated either with woven design with embroidery. These ornemented ends were called 'zabir'. The edges were sometimes also ornamented, usually with simple woven designs. Here are some women Horoden'ka district costume wearing peremitky.
This type embroidery was used women's chemises well, but lasted longest the zabory because could made look almost the same both sides.
The image the top the article good example this technique, even though the photo somewhat washed out. will using several images which are rather poor quality, and apologize that was not able find better.
The framework for these designs was laid out black cross stich, sometimes stem stich. For the zabir, double sided cross stitch was used. Then colors were filled with herringbone pine type stitch, which started from the center line the outlined area and extended diagonally the outer edge. This was often executed silk thread, and sometimes metallic thread accent..Some the outlines, and often decorative elements the edge the design were done with Holbein stitch.Other stitches were used for the edging, including buttonhole and chain stitch.
Elements which are equilateral end looking almost like eyelets, but they are not pulled open. The primary colors used are dark reds, edging into maroon and purple with other colors used accents.
Some designs use stem stitch instead cross stitch, and are curvilinear. The filling done the same way however.
Many designs are symmetrical about the diagonal axis, which similar embroidery designs the Balkans.
And course, wherever creative embroiderers existed, variations the technique also existed.
More recently, this technique, with many others, was imitated with cross-stitch. Here are two shoulder insets from women's chemises with the same design, one done rozchokhy, and the other cross stitch.
And another pair, from zabory.
fact, belief that many designs this area with strong black outlines individual motifs which are today only found cross stitch were originally executed rozchokha. would interesting reverse engineer these designs.
suppose should mention that some these designs were deliberately left unfinished more clearly show the structure.
Occasionally you will see remnant rozchokha cross stitch design.
This technique has today been revived, and being taught places like the Uchylyshche L'viw. New designs using the technique are also being invented. had such piece personal collection, but was borrowed and never returned.
This beautiful part the creative heritage Ukraine and the world, and would encourage you try it. This technique has great deal potential.
Thank you for reading. will close with some more images pieces done this technique.
Hello, Roman. Thanks for this post! thought, that Pokuttya peremitkas was mainly woven (like this from Jaseniv-Pilny but your post opened eyes the wonderful local version embroidered peremitkas. don't have the 3rd Iryna Svyontek's book, ask you, there the book how this embroidery?
the way, "some women Horoden'ka district costume wearing peremitky" are the folk band "Peremitka" from village Luka, Horodenka raion and you can watch their performance youtube
Thank you, Nawko, would like know where could find her other two books. have not found them available here.
She does not show how this embroidery, but you can save and enlarge the black and white images and able see how done.It pretty cross stitch and outline stitch, filling with herringbone sosnytsia. Most books Ukrainian embroidery cover this, for example the one Diakiw neil. called plait stitch, peremitkovy show Horodenkovy stib, somesuch. There description, diagrams 'Podil'ska Narodna Vyshywka Liudmyla
Thank you for your information. and please let know how could buy her other two books.
Перші присвячені Гуцульщині, одну них маю книжкою привезли іншу найстаршу скановану копію форматі djvu. Києві видно продажу, видно
Нагадують книжку Сороханюк, але жаль, гіршої якості. маєте знати, поліграфічна якість книжок пані Свйонтек покращувалась кожним виданням та, маєте найкраща, давніші трохи поступаються.
знайшла видавництво, видало дві трьох книжок, "Нова Зоря", ними можна сконтактувати електронною поштою nz@com.ua.
Також, ймовірно, усі книжки авторки, але контактів нажаль маю.
один коментар українською, усе, хочу, моя погана англійська дозволить написати (сподіваюсь, мене зрозумієте). білі вишивки Житомирщини. Суємці навколишні) наполегливо шукаю книги статті, виділяли вишивку, але поки марно. Єдине джерело, містить замальовки "Український народний орнамент" Олени Пчілки, 1876 (починаючи аркуша). Завдяки вишивальниці Тетяні можемо переглядати видання онлайн тут Цей альбом містить зразки орнаментів повіту, якого Баранівський район належав, але містить конкретики щодо місць побутування кожного окремого узору також мість дуже загальний опис вишивки вирізуванням настилуванням без виділення якихось локальних рис.
Усе інше, вдалось знайти фото вишивок Мережі. Велику підбірку фондах музею Гончара пані Олена Уткіна. фото можна знайти альбомі іншої вишивальниці, пані Лідії Бебешко, яка перетворює PM-схеми:
Усі інші зразки, мені вдалось знайти, зібрала дошку Pinterest: (звідти можна потрапити першоджерела, якщо були соціальні медіа).
Pereproshuju shcho vam vidpyshu latinikamy. chudovi znymky knyshchi 'Vyshywka Skhidnoho Podillia', pysaly Jevhen Tetiana Prychepi, vydawnytsa 'Rodovid'.
Але тут мене зацікавило Житомирське біле шитво, оремий різновид українських вишивок білим білому. скільки бачу воно якраз мало схоже вінницьке снятинське, більше схоже полтавське, хоча має ряд особливостей, які якраз пунктиками :), хоча мало бути більше схоже рівненське (сарненський район), туди ближче.
majete uvazi Podil'ske vyrizuvannia. Tak, maju kil'ky fotohrafii tseho takozh. Mih vam posylaty jakscho meni daiete adress email.
Interesting and exhaustive articles here,thank you!
very modern view things you have. Embroidery Ukraine many other places was considered essential. was, and some extent still is, visible mark your good taste, how marriageable you are, and evidence proper householding skills. Untill about the 1930's, all the cloth and thread for embroidered items were made home, hand. Today pay exorbitant prices for good linen, but those days, was much more available. Even when commercial thread began used, was never that expensive. Secondly, these clothes were not worn for season and then discarded, unfortunately today. Linen extremely durable, and embroidered garment could worn for decades. This especially true for the most extensively embroidered garments which were only worn for important occasions, perhaps for church Sunday, most. Everyday clothes would eventually wear out, and had less embroidery, but even then would recycled footcloths, baby swaddling and the like. This was not much done city women untill the 1930's when became fashionable have ethnic identity. The woman the villages worked the family farm, and for much the year agricultural work does leave one with great deal free time, excepting only planting time and harvest. Also, one the most important things that young girls were expected was prepare their trousseau; girl was not considered ready for marriage untill she had proper collection embroidered clothes and household linens prepared, often including shirt for her future spouse. Women would also embroider shirts for their husbands and sons. was considered extremely strong protective talisman for young man out into the world with shirt embroidered his mother. Embroidery was never considered just hobby', formed vital part life. This has not completely been lost Ukrainian culture even today. And course, all this applies many other peoples well.
Ohh,thank you, Roman; very informative.
Hi.. Great post. Well you have discussed here few things about Costume Embroidery and your images also looks amazing. Keep this type posting.

original text:
The landscape development finance changing rapidly. Traditionally, international financial flows moved from developed countries developing countries. the last decade, however, major emerging economies such China and Brazil have fueled growing trend South-South development flows increasingly channeling their overseas investments other developing countries.
China and Brazil are surfacing major international investors through nationally owned financial institutions such the Export-Import Bank China, the China Development Bank and the Brazilian Development Bank (BNDES). These “emerging actors” are financing major initiatives acquire natural resources, open markets, and forge strategic political ties. They are increasingly financing large-scale, high impact projects beyond their borders—such hydropower plants and gas may pose new challenges for environmental and social
This preliminary research focuses Chinese and Brazilian overseas investments and begins look the growth drivers and geographic trends those investments.
Expanding South-South trade and investment provides welcome and needed sources capital for countries Africa, Asia, and Latin America. the same time, these financial flows coupled with the emergence powerful financial actors from China, India, Brazil, and other economies may pose new challenges for environmental and social
Trends indicate that developing economies will “account for 57% world GDP [Gross Domestic Product] 2030.”3 Despite sharp differences among members, the G-20 supplanting the G-8 the primary vehicle for global economic policy coordination.4 Large emerging market economies are defining their own approaches development cooperation, governance issues, and environmental and social outside many existing normative frameworks.
2001, China’s tenth Five-Year Plan (2001-2005) formalized the directive for Chinese companies “Go Global,” strategy gain access needed resources, stimulate the export goods, and grow China’s multinational businesses and brands. Beijing has provided diplomatic support, favorable tax exemptions, insurance, and, critically, access low-cost finance.
Chinese authorities have simplified regulations facilitate investment abroad. Three governmental bodies MOFCOM, SAFE, and the NDRC have primary but not sole oversight China’s overseas investment (separate from foreign assistance) regime. MOFCOM responsible for developing regulations for outbound investment and for coordinating activities with commercial counselors posted Chinese embassies. SAFE issued new regulations 2009 that reduced qualification requirements for offshore foreign currency lending and expanded the sources funds for lending (including access government foreign exchange reserves).11 The NDRC reviews large outbound investments ensure they align with the country’s political interest and overall economic development policy.
addition, CBRC and SASAC also play oversight role. Risk management guidelines issued the CBRC 2008 opened the door for Chinese banks provide loans for merger and acquisition purposes (previously forbidden under 1996 regulation.) They require “banks perform due diligence regarding compliance, operational, and commercial risks relating the parties and the transaction.”12 There mention social and environmental risks.
Rather than seeking financing primarily through the capital markets, Chinese companies obtain 80-90% their funding from Chinese banks.13 part the Global strategy, China’s state-owned policy banks, largely the Export-Import Bank China (China Exim) and the China Development Bank (CDB), were mobilized facilitate international capital flows and support mergers and acquisitions foreign companies. Although not the largest terms total assets and domestic investment, China Exim Bank and CDB play the leading role overseas investment. Other state-owned banks, such the export and credit insurance company (Sinosure), have also contributed lesser scale.
The Export-Import Bank China (China Exim) was formed 1994 along with two other “policy banks,” the China Development Bank and the Agricultural Development Bank China, “as tools the government, allowing Beijing allocate preferential targeted finance through hybrid planning and market means.”14 policy bank, China Exim finances and implements the government’s trade and overseas investment policies.15 The Bank under the direct leadership the State Council.
China Exim has exhibited phenomenal growth over the past decade. has increased lending volumes 30% 40% year-on-year indicator the accelerating nature the "Go Global" strategy. China Exim far the largest export credit agency the world. approved over $70 billion new lending 2009, more than U.S. Exim, JBIC, and BNDES Exim combined.
Why China Going Global?
Increasing demand for energy and natural resources major driver behind China’s foreign direct investments. 2010, China was the world's leading consumer several major commodities including copper, steel, coal, lead and iron ore. China’s economy experiences unprecedented growth, there growing need for new markets, technology, and brands.
The bulk China’s imports from Africa originate from relatively few countries. While investments are spread across African countries, over 70% Chinese OFDI stock 2008 was concentrated five resource rich countries: South Africa, Nigeria, Zambia, Sudan, and Algeria.

(4.9%).23 The top three African oil exporters Nigeria, Angola, Algeria for the U.S. and Angola, Sudan, Libya for China provided quarter each country’s total imports (27.6% the U.S., 25.1% for China).

China and Brazil seek similar relationships with Africa, sharing strong proclivity oil and mining activities. This makes less likely that the two countries will compete over investment opportunities. key challenge ensure these trade flows result clear benefits the host government and local communities, and safeguard the environment.
With our record independent research, our experience convening wide range stakeholders, and our close partnerships with organizations China, Brazil, and several host countries Africa and Asia, WRI's International Financial Flows and the Environment (IFFE) initiative supports efforts both investors and host countries move towards and socially responsible development.
IFFE works help these countries apply the highest climate change, environmental and social standards their investment overseas, report publicly these standards, and respond the concerns NGOs and local communities.
For additional information WRI’s Emerging Actors Development Finance work, contact WRI Senior Associate Xiaomei Tan xtan@wri.org.
Note: All currencies are quotes U.S. dollars unless otherwise stated.
This collection figures and charts based preliminary research conducted Bruce Jenkins, WRI consultant, and Xiaomei Tan, WRI Senior Associate. The scoping research concluded April 2011 and includes data from various sources that are updated frequently. The data circulated stimulate timely discussion and critical feedback and influence ongoing debate emerging issues. WRI has not verified the data, and figures and charts are meant used for illustrative purposes. WRI will continue update the data our research moves forward.

2001, China’s tenth Five-Year Plan (2001-2005) formalized the directive for Chinese companies “Go Global,” strategy gain access needed resources, stimulate the export goods, and grow China’s multinational businesses and brands. Beijing has provided diplomatic support, favorable tax exemptions, insurance, and, critically, access low-cost finance.
Chinese authorities have simplified regulations facilitate investment abroad. Three governmental bodies MOFCOM, SAFE, and the NDRC have primary but not sole oversight China’s overseas investment (separate from foreign assistance) regime. MOFCOM responsible for developing regulations for outbound investment and for coordinating activities with commercial counselors posted Chinese embassies. SAFE issued new regulations 2009 that reduced qualification requirements for offshore foreign currency lending and expanded the sources funds for lending (including access government foreign exchange reserves).11 The NDRC reviews large outbound investments ensure they align with the country’s political interest and overall economic development policy.
addition, CBRC and SASAC also play oversight role. Risk management guidelines issued the CBRC 2008 opened the door for Chinese banks provide loans for merger and acquisition purposes (previously forbidden under 1996 regulation.) They require “banks perform due diligence regarding compliance, operational, and commercial risks relating the parties and the transaction.”12 There mention social and environmental risks.
Rather than seeking financing primarily through the capital markets, Chinese companies obtain 80-90% their funding from Chinese banks.13 part the Global strategy, China’s state-owned policy banks, largely the Export-Import Bank China (China Exim) and the China Development Bank (CDB), were mobilized facilitate international capital flows and support mergers and acquisitions foreign companies. Although not the largest terms total assets and domestic investment, China Exim Bank and CDB play the leading role overseas investment. Other state-owned banks, such the export and credit insurance company (Sinosure), have also contributed lesser scale.
The Export-Import Bank China (China Exim) was formed 1994 along with two other “policy banks,” the China Development Bank and the Agricultural Development Bank China, “as tools the government, allowing Beijing allocate preferential targeted finance through hybrid planning and market means.”14 policy bank, China Exim finances and implements the government’s trade and overseas investment policies.15 The Bank under the direct leadership the State Council.
China Exim has exhibited phenomenal growth over the past decade. has increased lending volumes 30% 40% year-on-year indicator the accelerating nature the "Go Global" strategy. China Exim far the largest export credit agency the world. approved over $70 billion new lending 2009, more than U.S. Exim, JBIC, and BNDES Exim combined.

The Department Communication pleased announce its new Graduate Certificate Professional Studies Professional Communication for Enhanced English Fluency (EEF), offered the University Maryland, College Park.
You can pursue this program online -campus. Individuals can pursue the program part time full time. available and international applicants (eef@umd.edu).
Department Communication faculty members and graduate students will present research and participate meetings the 102nd annual convention the National Communication Association, November 10-13, 2016 Philadelphia. Read more here.
Recently published the Journal International and Intercultural Communication: "Crossing the Communication Divide: Intergroup Contact and the Desire Learn the Outgroup's Language," co-authored Assistant Professor Nick Joyce.
